Package component.modifier

Class Modifier

java.lang.Object
component.modifier.Modifier
All Implemented Interfaces:
Tippable, GridIndexable
Direct Known Subclasses:
Changer, Combinator, Pathway
public abstract class Modifier extends Object implements GridIndexable, Tippable
The Modifier class is the abstract foundation for all interactive board components.

It manages the physical location on the grid, the operational state (active/disabled), and universal card material interactions like Corruption and Fragility.

    Default constructor for a Modifier.

  • Constructor Details

    • Modifier

      public Modifier()
      Default constructor for a Modifier.

      Initializes the modifier at a default GridPos (typically 0,0) and sets its state to enabled. This is primarily used for temporary instances or when the position will be set immediately after instantiation.

    • Modifier

      public Modifier(GridPos gridPos)
      Constructs a Modifier at a specific location on the game grid.

      This constructor handles the internal initialization of the position object and ensures the modifier starts in an active (non-disabled) state. The provided coordinates are validated via setGridPos(GridPos). * @param gridPos The initial GridPos where this modifier will be placed on the level board.

  • Method Details

    • modify

      public abstract void modify(Card toModify)
      Executes the specific modification logic for this tile. Must be implemented by subclasses.
      Parameters:
      toModify - The card currently interacting with the tile.

    • reset

      public abstract void reset()
      Resets the modifier to its initial level-start state.

    • onSuccess

      public void onSuccess()
      Records a successful card modification in the current game level. This is used for animation triggers and level state tracking.

    • checkSetDisable

      public boolean checkSetDisable(Card card)
      Checks if the modifier is disabled or should be disabled by the card.

      Corruption Mechanic: If a Material.CORRUPTED card enters, it permanently disables this modifier and transforms itself into Material.PLASTIC.

      Parameters:
      card - The incoming card.
      Returns:
      true if the modifier is currently disabled.

    • checkDestroyGlass

      public boolean checkDestroyGlass(Card card)
      Manages the durability of cards (specifically Glass).

      Every time a card interacts with a modifier, its health decreases by 1. If health reaches 0, the card is removed from the game. Note: A health of -1 represents infinite durability.

      Parameters:
      card - The card to process.
      Returns:
      true if the card was destroyed (shattered).

    • isDisabled

      public boolean isDisabled()
      Checks the current operational status of the modifier.
      Returns:
      true if the modifier is disabled (e.g., by Corruption), false otherwise.

    • setDisabled

      public void setDisabled(boolean disabled)
      Explicitly sets the operational status of the modifier.
      Parameters:
      disabled - The new disabled state.

    • getGridPos

      public GridPos getGridPos()
      Retrieves the current grid coordinates of this modifier.
      Specified by:
      getGridPos in interface GridIndexable
      Returns:
      A GridPos object representing the tile location.

    • setGridPos

      public void setGridPos(GridPos point)
      Updates the modifier's position on the board.

      The input coordinates are clamped between 0 and the maximum width/height defined in GameLevel to prevent out-of-bounds placement.

      Specified by:
      setGridPos in interface GridIndexable
      Parameters:
      point - The target GridPos for the modifier.

    • getTooltip

      public Tooltip getTooltip()
      Returns the primary tooltip explaining the modifier's function.
      Specified by:
      getTooltip in interface Tippable
      Returns:
      A Tooltip object with descriptive text and theme colors.

    • getModifierTooltip

      public static Tooltip getModifierTooltip()
      Provides a static, generic tooltip for the abstract Modifier type. Used primarily for UI elements that reference the category as a whole.
      Returns:
      A default Tooltip for a generic machine tile.

    • getDisabledTooltip

      public Tooltip getDisabledTooltip()
      Provides a warning tooltip if the modifier has been sabotaged or disabled.
      Returns:
      A red "Disabled" Tooltip if isDisabled() is true, otherwise null.

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Returns the class name of the specific implementation.
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
      Returns:
      The simple name of the subclass (e.g., "Adder", "Vaporizer").
